pr.title: Bring progress site back from the dead
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Restored the project progress website by adding a new SvelteKit-based static site under tools/progress-site and wiring the GitHub Pages generation container to build and publish it at /progress. The PR also split Docker setup responsibilities so the build-linux image keeps only build dependencies while the gen-pages target installs Doxygen, Python tooling, Node 20, progress-site node_modules, external assets, fonts, and the mwcc compiler needed for ctx.c checking. Evidence is mostly from the diff; there was no PR body, issue discussion, review, or review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added a complete tools/progress-site SvelteKit project with package.json, package-lock.json, TypeScript config, Vite config, Svelte config, ESLint/Prettier config, README, AGPL license, and placeholder extern directories.
Implemented a static progress UI with App.svelte, Button/ButtonContainer components, a ProgressPanel component, global styling, and a Svelte store for the selected tab.

ProgressPanel.svelte imports progress JSON from $lib/extern/progress.json, interprets code and data measures from the GALE01 progress dataset, and renders a Highcharts line chart for code and data completion over recorded commits.
ProgressPanel tooltips link each point back to the doldecomp/melee commit hash and display derived trophy/event counts based on code/data percentages.
Configured SvelteKit static prerendering via src/routes/+layout.js and @sveltejs/adapter-static so the site can be emitted as static files for GitHub Pages.

Extended .github/packages/gen-pages/entrypoint.sh to build the old generated documentation outputs, run m2ctx/ctx.c checking, then build the progress site and move its build output to /output/progress.

Added .github/packages/gen-pages/setup.sh to install Node 20, Doxygen, Git, Python, external melee assets, an external Folk Pro font, node_modules for the progress site, the Python venv requirements, and the melee compiler used for ctx.c validation.
Changed the Dockerfile so setup.sh is no longer run in the shared linux-base stage; build-linux and gen-pages now copy and run their own setup scripts in separate targets.
Reduced build-linux/setup.sh to the packages needed for build-linux and removed its Python venv/pip installation, which moved to gen-pages/setup.sh.
Moved the GitHub Pages configure-pages action in publish-pages.yml to after the Docker generation step and before upload-pages-artifact.

Added a visible note in gen-pages/entrypoint.sh that fetching progress from https://progress.decomp.club/data/melee/GALE01?format=json&mode=all has a known race condition between build-melee and gen-pages.
smart_moves: Separated Docker target setup for build-linux versus gen-pages instead of bloating the shared base image with Node, Doxygen, Python venv, assets, and page-generation-only dependencies.

Preinstalled progress-site node_modules in the gen-pages setup stage, copied them into the checked-out repo, and used npm install --prefer-offline during page generation to make the runtime build faster and more reproducible.
Kept generated or external files out of the repository by using extern directories with .gitkeep plus .gitignore rules for **/extern/* while allowing CI to populate those assets at build time.
Used sparse checkout against doldecomp/assets to fetch only the melee asset subset needed by the progress site.
Kept the progress website static by configuring SvelteKit adapter-static and prerendering, which matches GitHub Pages deployment constraints.
Preserved existing pages generation duties, including Doxygen output and ctx.html generation, while adding /progress as a new output subtree rather than replacing the old output.
Added strict shell flags including -x in gen-pages scripts, improving CI log visibility for this relatively complex page-generation path.
decomp_lessons: Repository progress reporting can be treated as first-class decomp tooling: keep the site source in tools/ and build it in the same Pages generation pipeline that publishes Doxygen and ctx artifacts.
When CI images serve multiple roles, split target-specific setup into separate Docker stages to avoid unrelated dependency coupling and cache invalidation.
Do not commit downloaded assets or progress JSON; commit stable placeholders and have CI acquire assets/data during generation.

For static pages fed by decomp progress data, define a small typed interface for the dataset shape before using it in UI code; here Progress.ts records project/version/dol commits and code/data measure fields.
If a pipeline consumes an external progress service, document known freshness/race issues in the script where the fetch happens so future maintainers understand inconsistent output.
A Pages job can publish multiple decomp artifacts together: generated docs, ctx HTML, and progress visualization can all be placed under the same /output artifact root.
naming_conventions: The restored website lives under tools/progress-site, matching the repository pattern that project-specific tooling belongs under tools/.
Generated/external website assets are staged under static/extern and src/lib/extern, with .gitkeep placeholders and ignore rules for actual external contents.
The Pages Docker target is named gen-pages and has separate .github/packages/gen-pages/setup.sh and entrypoint.sh scripts.
The progress dataset type uses measure keys matching the external JSON schema, including code, code/total, data, and data/total.
Svelte components use PascalCase filenames such as ProgressPanel.svelte, Button.svelte, and ButtonContainer.svelte.
The selected tab store is named selectedOption and lives in src/store.js.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching or C decompilation tactics were introduced in this PR.

The gen-pages pipeline continues to run python tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py -pqr and compile build/ctx.c with melee-mwcc to produce ctx.html, so the page-generation container must still include the mwcc compiler even though the primary change is website tooling.
The progress chart derives code and data completion from progress-service measures rather than from local map parsing in this diff.
review_feedback: No review comments, reviews, or issue comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No PR body was present; intent is inferred from the title and diff only.
searchable_terms: Bring progress site back from the dead
race condition between build-melee and gen-pages
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify tools/progress-site or ProgressPanel.svelte to fix chart behavior, tests, or styling.
Find later PRs addressing the noted race condition between build-melee and gen-pages when fetching progress.decomp.club data.
Find CI failures or fixes related to .github/packages/gen-pages/setup.sh, NodeSource setup_20.x, external fonts, or doldecomp/assets sparse checkout.
Find later changes to publish-pages.yml ordering or GitHub Pages artifact layout.
Find PRs that replace the default create-svelte README or placeholder Playwright test expecting 'Welcome to SvelteKit'.
